evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential
Entity of type FAQPage has no @id — cross-page interconnection blocked
FAQPage schema has 10 Questions but page shows ~22 FAQ pairs
Post is 2219 words (pillar) — include a <nav aria-label="Table of contents"> for LLM outline extraction
Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice vs committee 6/10 — Some punchy lines ('someone will still be sulting about it'), but overall reads like a branded content team with editorial voice.
Genuine opinions vs safe claims 6/10 — Does take stances (Duke's is touristy and fine, skip Hanauma at your peril, WhatsApp is for ranting) so opinions come through.
Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — Has some fresh phrasing ('hangover half of the group', 'sand-to-terminal move'), but leans on tropes like 'world-class' and 'crowd-pleaser'.
Quotable sentence 5/10 — A couple of lines ('this is the job now') are quotable, but most sentences are functional.
Intro earns attention 5/10 — Generic hook followed by a meta-description of the article; doesn't create specificity or stakes fast.

specificity 6/10 — The '500 friend groups' statistic is unverifiable and vague—500 out of what universe? It reads as a made-up authority signal rather than grounded data. A second offender is 'group vacation rentals cutting costs (4–8 people split works best)'—'works best' floats without a dollar anchor until the budget section. The individual day entries are admirably specific (prices, distances, names), but this stat undercuts the otherwise concrete tone.
